pattern: Casting a ray outward from the king along each of the eight directions and stopping at the first occupied square reduces the attack test to a per-direction nearest-blocker scan.
hints: A queen threatens the king only along a straight line: same row, same column, or a 45-degree diagonal.
Along any one of the eight directions, only the closest queen matters, because she blocks every queen behind her.
Step outward from the king one square at a time in each of the eight directions; the first queen you meet in a direction is an attacker, then move on to the next direction.

edge_inputs.description: king in a corner with attackers on several rays
diagonal attackers around a central king
single queen directly attacking on a column
single queen that does not attack at all
a nearer queen shields a farther one on the same ray
king fully surrounded yields exactly eight attackers

stress.description: random queens on the 8x8 board with a random king square
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    cells = [(x, y) for x in range(8) for y in range(8)]
    rng.shuffle(cells)
    kx, ky = cells.pop()
    k = rng.randint(1, min(size, len(cells)))
    queens = [[x, y] for (x, y) in cells[:k]]
    return [queens, [kx, ky]]
stress.note: king square is removed from the pool so positions stay unique
